rulings.Case Title: ST. PAULUS EVANGELICAL LUTHERAN CHURCH, A VS. MERKER ARCHITECTURAL GROUP INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Pretrial Discovery Of Defendants Financial Condition
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Thursday, December 04, 2025, Line 1. PLAINTIFF ST. PAULUS EVANGELICAL LUTHERAN CHURCH's Motion For Pretrial Discovery Of Defendants Financial Condition. Plaintiff St. Paulus Evangelical Lutheran Church's unopposed Motion For Pretrial Discovery Of Defendants Financial Condition is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. (The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties). For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: EMI J. YOSHIDA ET AL VS. 479 LOS PALMOS, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Thursday, December 04, 2025, Line 2. 1 - DEFENDANTs 479 LOS PALMOS, LLC, and JOHN NASH's MOTION TO QUASH SUBPOENAS. Defendants 479 Los Palmos, LLC and John H. Nash's Motion to Quash Plaintiffs' Subpoena for Production of Business Records (Bank Records) is DENIED. (The Court's complete tentative ruling has been emailed to the parties). Moving Defendants did not demonstrate that the subpoenas are overbroad. The record demonstrates that Plaintiffs' subpoeanas are appropriately tailored in scope. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: CITIBANK, N.A. VS. AURA G ALVAREZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order That Matters In Request For Admission Of Truth Of Facts Be Deemed Admitted; Memorandum In Support Of Motion; Declaration In Support Of Motion
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Thursday, December 04, 2025, Line 6. PLAINTIFF CITIBANK, N.A.'s Motion For Order That Matters In Request For Admission Of Truth Of Facts Be Deemed Admitted. Plaintiff Citibank, N.A.'s unopposed Motion For Order That Matters In Request For Admissions Be Deemed Admitted is GRANTED. The record demonstrates Defendant Aura G. Alvarez did not provide any responses to Plaintiff's Request For Admissions. Set One. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 2033.280.) Defendant's failure to timely respond is not excused. No meet and confer requirement when no responses have been provided. Plaintiffs' RFAs, Set One are deemed admitted. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts.
